summary: Iteration 38 - VA PDF Report Generation feature testing complete. Backend: 11/11 tests passed (100%). Frontend: Report PDF button present and functional, PDF download works correctly (27KB file). All existing VA functionality (dashboard, remote scan, tabs) still working.

backend_api: GET /api/vulnerability/report/{client_id} - Content-Disposition has attachment and filename.pdf
GET /api/vulnerability/report/{client_id} - PDF starts with %PDF- magic bytes
frontend_ui: Report PDF button [data-testid='va-download-report-btn'] present with text 'Report PDF'
Report PDF button is enabled when data is loaded
Report PDF button triggers PDF download when clicked
Remote Scan button [data-testid='va-run-scan-btn'] still present with text 'Avvia Scansione Remota'
Stats section [data-testid='va-stats'] shows 13 devices, 22 vulns, 8 critical, 2 high

critical_code_review_comments: vulnerability.py: PDF report endpoint correctly uses Depends(get_current_user) for auth
vulnerability.py: PDF uses ReportLab with proper Italian text and 11 sections

vulnerability.py: PDF includes all required sections: cover, executive summary, severity distribution, device analysis, critical vulns, full vuln list, detailed analysis, remediation plan, scan history, recommendations, methodology, knowledge base
vulnerability.py: PDF filename includes client name and date
vulnerability.py: StreamingResponse correctly sets Content-Type and Content-Disposition headers
VulnerabilityPage.js: downloadReport function uses fetch with auth headers
VulnerabilityPage.js: Creates blob URL and triggers download with correct filename

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all UI components verified, PDF download works)

seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Using existing demo data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 38 verified the NEW PDF report generation feature. The endpoint GET /api/vulnerability/report/{client_id} generates a comprehensive 27KB PDF with 11 sections. Frontend has 'Report PDF' button next to 'Avvia Scansione Remota' that triggers authenticated download. PDF filename format: VA_Report_{client_name}_{date}.pdf. All previous VA functionality (dashboard, remote scan, tabs) still working.

pdf_sections: 4. Vulnerabilità Critiche e Alte (Critical & High Vulns)
11. Knowledge Base Porte Pericolose (Risky Ports KB)

frontend_download_button.behavior: Triggers authenticated fetch and blob download
